items.title: Add OpenClaw preflight to detect broken Telegram extension paths
items.content:
### Motivation
- Reduce intermittent runbook failures where `status-all` crashes with ENOENT due to stale Telegram extension references in `openclaw.json`.
- Provide a simple, repeatable preflight that operators can run before nightly runbooks to catch broken plugin paths.

### Description
- Add `bin/openclaw-preflight.php`, an executable CLI checker that reads `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` (or a provided path) and scans for Telegram `channel.setup.js` entries and verifies the resolved files exist.
- The checker returns `0` for OK, `1` when broken Telegram references are found (prints remediation guidance), and `2` for hard errors like missing/unreadable/invalid config.
- Document the preflight and usage in `README.md` under a new **Preflight operativo (OpenClaw runbooks)** section.
- The script includes path resolution for relative entries and flattens JSON to report the exact key/value pairs with resolved filesystem paths.

### Testing
- Ran `php -l bin/openclaw-preflight.php` to verify syntax; result: no syntax errors (success).
- Ran the script against a missing config path (`php bin/openclaw-preflight.php /tmp/does-not-exist.json`); observed controlled error output and exit code `2` (success).
- End-to-end temp-config test: created a temporary `openclaw.json` referencing `./dist/extensions/telegram/src/channel.setup.js` and ran the checker to observe `EXIT:1` when file was absent and `EXIT:0` after creating the referenced file (success).

items.content:
## Signal

**Earendil Inc.** launched today (Apr 8, 2026). Founded by Armin Ronacher (creator of Flask, Rye, UV — some of the most important Python tooling of the last decade) and Colin Daymond Hanna.

**Pi** — minimal agent within OpenClaw, widely-adopted open source coding agent and harness. Earendil acquired Pi and Mario Zechner (its creator) became a major stakeholder.

**Lefos** — public alpha now. "A machine entity that proves both maximally capable and trustworthy. Lefos communicates with you and your co-creators over email, a stack that you control and where you can collaborate with others. Lefos helps your inbox become a command line for thoughtful work and communication."

**Backers:** Accel (Daniel Levine), Balderton (Daniel Waterhouse), founders of n8n, OpenClaw, Revolut (Vlad Yatsenko), Sentry (David Cramer), Slack (Cal Henderson).

Source: earendil.com/posts/press-release-april-8th/

## Why This Matters to 8GI

**1. Trust-first framing is our thesis.** Earendil's "maximally capable AND trustworthy" framing is identical to 8GI's governance thesis. They are pursuing it via email (a stack the user controls). We are pursuing it via local-first OS + network guard + activity log. Same problem, different surface.

**2. Armin Ronacher's credibility is significant.** He doesn't ship noise. Flask, Rye, UV are all tools developers actually use. Lefos will get serious developer attention.

**3. Email as command interface vs. Telegram.** Lefos uses email as the communication layer (user-controlled, auditable). 8gent uses Telegram. Both are trying to avoid proprietary cloud lock-in for AI communication. Worth watching which surface wins with which audience.

**4. Pi acquisition is the pattern play.** Acquiring a proven open-source coding agent (rather than building from scratch) is a faster path to production capability. Monitor whether this becomes a pattern — established OS tools being acquired into AI agent platforms.

🤖 *This is an automated pull request from Repo Assist.*

## Summary

This PR covers two improvements:

**Task 5 – Coding Improvement:** Adds a `GetStringArrayArg` protected helper to `NodeCapabilityBase` and uses it to simplify `SystemCapability.HandleWhich`.

**Task 9 – Testing Improvements:** Adds 8 new unit tests for `WindowsNodeClient` and 6 new tests for the new `GetStringArrayArg` helper (14 net-new tests).

## Changes

### `NodeCapabilityBase.GetStringArrayArg` (new helper)

`NodeCapabilityBase` already has `GetStringArg`, `GetIntArg`, and `GetBoolArg` helpers. This PR adds the natural companion:

```csharp
protected string[] GetStringArrayArg(JsonElement args, string name)
```

- Returns `Array.Empty(string)()` when the property is absent, the args element is default, or the property is not a JSON array.
- Filters out `null` and whitespace-only strings from the array.
- Handles mixed-type arrays safely (skips non-string elements).

### `SystemCapability.HandleWhich` simplified

Replaces 22 lines of manual JSON array parsing (with verbose `System.Text.Json.JsonValueKind.*` references) with a single call to `GetStringArrayArg`:

```csharp
// Before: 22 lines of manual parsing
var bins = new List(string)();
if (request.Args.ValueKind != System.Text.Json.JsonValueKind.Undefined && ...)
{ ... }

// After: 1 line
var bins = GetStringArrayArg(request.Args, "bins");
```

Behaviour is identical; the helper uses `string[]` instead of `List(string)` so `bins.Length` replaces `bins.Count` in the guard.

**`NodeCapabilitiesTests`** – 6 new tests for `GetStringArrayArg`:
- Returns values from a valid array
- Returns empty when property is missing

- Filters out whitespace-only elements

items.title: ⚡ Bolt: optimize MMR re-ranking from O(N³) to O(N²)
items.content:
Optimized the MMR re-ranking algorithm in `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/mmr.ts` from $O(N^3)$ to $O(N^2)$ by maintaining a cache of maximum similarities to the selected set.

### 💡 What
Modified `mmrRerank` to incrementally update the maximum similarity for each candidate item whenever a new item is added to the selected set. This replaces the previous approach of recalculating similarity to all selected items for every candidate in every iteration.

### 🎯 Why
The original implementation had a selection loop ($O(N)$ iterations) containing a candidate loop ($O(N)$ candidates) which called `maxSimilarityToSelected`. `maxSimilarityToSelected` itself iterated over all already selected items ($O(N)$), resulting in $O(N^3)$ total similarity comparisons.

### 📊 Impact
Benchmark results for `mmrRerank` with random items:
- **100 items:** 94ms -> 10ms (~9x faster)
- **500 items:** 13.5s -> 100ms (~135x faster)
- **1000 items:** 109.5s -> 360ms (~300x faster)

### 🔬 Measurement
1. Run `npx vitest run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/mmr.test.ts` to ensure correctness.
2. Performance can be verified by running a benchmark with 1000 items before and after the change.

Journal entry added to `.jules/bolt.md`.

items.content:
## Summary

Adds **Krea AI** as a bundled skill, giving OpenClaw access to the [Krea](https://krea.ai) creative AI platform:

- **Image generation** — 20+ models (Flux, Imagen 4, GPT Image, Nano Banana, Ideogram, Seedream, and more)
- **Video generation** — 7 models (Veo 3, Kling, Hailuo, Wan)
- **Image enhancement/upscaling** — 3 Topaz upscalers (Standard, Generative, Bloom) up to 22K resolution
- **LoRA style training** — Train custom styles from 3–2000 images, then use them in generation
- **Multi-step pipelines** — Chain generation, enhancement, and video with fan_out branching, parallelism, resume, and template variables

All models are resolved dynamically from the Krea API's OpenAPI spec — no hardcoded endpoint tables. Scripts use `uv run` with PEP 723 inline dependencies (`requests` only).

## Structure

```
skills/krea-ai/
├── SKILL.md                    # Skill instructions with metadata.openclaw gating
├── scripts/
│   ├── krea_helpers.py         # Shared: API calls, retry, polling, caching, error formatting
│   ├── generate_image.py       # Image generation
│   ├── generate_video.py       # Video generation
│   ├── enhance_image.py        # Image upscaling/enhancement
│   ├── train_style.py          # LoRA style training
│   ├── list_models.py          # List available models from live API spec
│   ├── pipeline.py             # Multi-step pipeline runner
│   └── get_job.py              # Job status checker
└── references/
    ├── parameters.md           # Full parameter reference for all scripts
    ├── pipelines.md            # Pipeline format, examples, advanced features
    └── cookbook.md              # 5 real-world recipes
```

## Requirements

- **`uv`** — Python script runner (handles PEP 723 inline dependencies)
- **`KREA_API_TOKEN`** — API token from [krea.ai/settings/api-tokens](https://krea.ai/settings/api-tokens)

Gated via `metadata.openclaw`: `requires.bins: ["uv"]`, `requires.env: ["KREA_API_TOKEN"]`, `primaryEnv: "KREA_API_TOKEN"`.

items.title: feat: CLAUDE_PROXY_SAFE_MODE opt-out + document the RCE threat model
items.content:
## Summary
The proxy unconditionally launches the CLI with \`--dangerously-skip-permissions\`, which lets the model run arbitrary Bash without an interactive prompt. Combined with the default 127.0.0.1 bind, the only thing standing between an OpenClaw / browser extension / curl on the same host and arbitrary shell execution is the network boundary — and that was never spelled out anywhere.

Two changes:

1. **Opt-in safe mode.** \`CLAUDE_PROXY_SAFE_MODE=1\` makes \`buildArgs()\` drop \`--dangerously-skip-permissions\`, restoring the CLI's interactive permission prompts. Suitable for one-off interactive use; will hang most automated agent flows.

2. **Threat model documentation in README.** Names the danger explicitly, lists the do-not-do guidance (don't expose the port, don't run untrusted clients, CORS only stops drive-by browser tabs), documents the safe-mode env switch and its trade-off, and adds \`CLAUDE_PROXY_SAFE_MODE\` and \`CLAUDE_PROXY_ALLOW_CORS_ANY\` to the env-var table.

No code path changes for the default deployment; safe mode is purely opt-in.

## Test plan
- [x] \`npm run build\`
- [x] \`npm test\` — 19 pass
- [ ] Run with \`CLAUDE_PROXY_SAFE_MODE=1\` and confirm the CLI prompts for tool permissions

items.title: [Release] openclaw 2026.4.9
items.content:
### Changes

- Memory/dreaming: add a grounded REM backfill lane with historical `rem-harness --path`, diary commit/reset flows, cleaner durable-fact extraction, and live short-term promotion integration so old daily notes can replay into Dreams and durable memory without a second memory stack. Thanks @mbelinky.
- Control UI/dreaming: add a structured diary view with timeline navigation, backfill/reset controls, traceable dreaming summaries, and a grounded Scene lane with promotion hints plus a safe clear-grounded action for staged backfill signals. (#63395) Thanks @mbelinky.
- QA/lab: add character-vibes evaluation reports with model selection and parallel runs so live QA can compare candidate behavior faster.
- Plugins/provider-auth: let provider manifests declare `providerAuthAliases` so provider variants can share env vars, auth profiles, config-backed auth, and API-key onboarding choices without core-specific wiring.
- iOS: pin release versioning to an explicit CalVer in `apps/ios/version.json`, keep TestFlight iteration on the same short version until maintainers intentionally promote the next gateway version, and add the documented `pnpm ios:version:pin -- --from-gateway` workflow for release trains. (#63001) Thanks @ngutman.

### Fixes

- Browser/security: re-run blocked-destination safety checks after interaction-driven main-frame navigations from click, evaluate, hook-triggered click, and batched action flows, so browser interactions cannot bypass the SSRF quarantine when they land on forbidden URLs. (#63226) Thanks @eleqtrizit.
- Security/dotenv: block runtime-control env vars plus browser-control override and skip-server env vars from untrusted workspace `.env` files, and reject unsafe URL-style browser control override specifiers before lazy loading. (#62660, #62663) Thanks @eleqtrizit.
- Gateway/node exec events: mark remote node `exec.started`, `exec.finished`, and `exec.denied` summaries as untrusted system events and sanitize node-provided command/o

items.title: [Release] openclaw 2026.4.8
items.content:
### Fixes

- Telegram/setup: load setup and secret contracts through packaged top-level sidecars so installed npm builds no longer try to import missing `dist/extensions/telegram/src/*` files during gateway startup.
- Bundled channels/setup: load shared secret contracts through packaged top-level sidecars across BlueBubbles, Feishu, Google Chat, IRC, Matrix, Mattermost, Microsoft Teams, Nextcloud Talk, Slack, and Zalo so installed npm builds no longer rely on missing `dist/extensions/*/src/*` files during gateway startup.
- Bundled plugins: align packaged plugin compatibility metadata with the release version so bundled channels and providers load on OpenClaw 2026.4.8.
- Agents/progress: keep `update_plan` available for OpenAI-family runs while returning compact success payloads and allowing `tools.experimental.planTool=false` to opt out.
- Agents/exec: keep `/exec` current-default reporting aligned with real runtime behavior so `host=auto` sessions surface the correct host-aware fallback policy (`full/off` on gateway or node, `deny/off` on sandbox) instead of stale stricter defaults.
- Slack: honor ambient HTTP(S) proxy settings for Socket Mode WebSocket connections, including NO_PROXY exclusions, so proxy-only deployments can connect without a monkey patch. (#62878) Thanks @mjamiv.
- Slack/actions: pass the already resolved read token into `downloadFile` so SecretRef-backed bot tokens no longer fail after a raw config re-read. (#62097) Thanks @martingarramon.
- Network/fetch guard: skip target DNS pinning when trusted env-proxy mode is active so proxy-only sandboxes can let the trusted proxy resolve outbound hosts. (#59007) Thanks @cluster2600.
